_publications._publicationTitle: Interpreting mammalian target of rapamycin and cell growth inhibition in a genetically engineered mouse model of Nf1-deficient astrocytes.

_contextSnippet: we found that 0.75nM rapamycin resulted in a 5-fold increase in AKT activation compared to vehicle control, whereas no change in AKT Ser 473 phosphorylation was observed at 0.001nM or 10nM rapamycin
resourceName: K4622 grade II glioma cell line
details: 0.75nM rapamycin resulted in 5-fold increase in AKT activation compared to vehicle control, with no change at 0.001nM or 10nM rapamycin
